definition: The Merton/KMV model explains 50-70% of cross-sectional CDS spread variation but is necessary-but-insufficient for credit-equity linkage: systematic Type I errors of 40-60%, underpredicts IG spreads by 50-100bp (credit spread puzzle), and is mechanically irrelevant for ~35% of S&P 500 index weight held by net-cash mega-caps. Useful for within-regime relative value but dangerous if treated as complete, particularly at regime transitions where it fails precisely when needed most.
evidence: KMV-Merton explains 50-70% of cross-sectional CDS spread variation (Bharath & Shumway 2008)
Credit spread puzzle: underpredicts IG by 50-100bp
Mechanically irrelevant for ~35% of S&P 500 held by net-cash mega-caps
All debates converged on useful foundation, dangerous if treated as complete
open_questions: What combination of Merton and Minsky/regime frameworks produces optimal credit-equity signal?
How should the model be adapted for the covenant-lite, addback-distorted universe?
